purpose: Remediate the L-2 preregistration before execution by aligning the matched-horizon primary utility, DSR input, and per-period MinTRL inference without changing the locked v1 artifact.
owner_authorization: The owner explicitly authorized B5.1 on 2026-07-24 to create a superseding L-2 preregistration gate, with no backtest, validation access, broker/provider call, or paid action.

superseded_artifact.remediation: v1 passed annualized Sharpe values directly to a per-period MinTRL kernel and used the 60-day L-1 baseline as the primary comparator despite the matched-horizon L-2 research question.

baseline_inheritance.comparison_rule: Candidate and every comparator use identical inherited universe, availability timestamps, one-session execution delay, sizing, rebalancing, cost model, return accounting, dates, and missingness handling. Only the signal transform differs.

candidate_signal.name: equal_weight_multi_lookback_t_stat
candidate_signal.component_formula: For each asset and horizon h, t_stat_h[t] = mean of the h prior available daily economic returns divided by their sample standard deviation times sqrt(h); require h complete observations and use no return at or after t.
candidate_signal.component_zero_variance_rule: Set that component to 0 and record the event; do not substitute an epsilon or drop the asset.
candidate_signal.combined_signal_formula: q_tstat[t] = arithmetic mean of tanh(t_stat_h[t]) across the four locked horizons.
candidate_signal.same_signal_availability_rule: The candidate signal is frozen before the inherited one-session execution delay; same-close execution is forbidden.

primary.name: equal_weight_multi_lookback_directional_count
primary.component_formula: q_count_h[t] = sum(sign(r[t-k]) for k=0..h-1) / h, using the inherited directional-count zero and missing-return rules.
primary.combined_signal_formula: q_count_matched[t] = arithmetic mean of q_count_h[t] across the four locked horizons.
primary.role: Primary comparator. It matches every candidate horizon and all inherited portfolio plumbing; it differs only by using directional counts instead of t-stat components.

secondary.role: Secondary descriptive reference only. Its 60-day single-lookback return difference is reported on identical dates but cannot replace the primary utility, MinTRL, PSR, DSR, or decision rule.

primary_utility_and_inference.observation_unit: paired same-date daily net active return: candidate net portfolio return minus primary matched-horizon comparator net portfolio return
primary_utility_and_inference.primary_utility: annualized Sharpe of the paired daily net active-return series
primary_utility_and_inference.annual_to_per_period_conversion: per_period_Sharpe = annualized_Sharpe / sqrt(252) before every MinTRL, PSR, or DSR calculation
primary_utility_and_inference.primary_margin_PSR_requirement: PSR on the paired daily net active-return series against the minimum_useful_improvement_per_period_sharpe is at least 0.95.
primary_utility_and_inference.secondary_diagnostic: Candidate turnover must not exceed the matched-horizon comparator turnover by more than 20 percent; this diagnostic cannot replace the primary utility.
primary_utility_and_inference.L1_secondary_reference: Report the annualized Sharpe of candidate minus the L-1 60-day baseline on identical dates descriptively only; no L-1-referenced statistic supports an L-2 decision.

search_accounting_and_DSR.DSR_input_series: For every locked trial, use its paired same-date daily net active-return series: t-stat candidate net portfolio return minus that trial's matched-horizon directional-count comparator net portfolio return.
search_accounting_and_DSR.effective_trials_for_DSR: maximum of 5 and the effective-rank estimate from the complete locked trial active-return correlation matrix
search_accounting_and_DSR.trial_sharpe_dispersion: Population standard deviation of per-period Sharpe estimates across the complete locked trial active-return inventory on the evaluated window; no omitted failed trial.
search_accounting_and_DSR.search_aware_null_per_period: minimum_useful_improvement_per_period_sharpe plus expected_maximum_sharpe from the complete trial active-return inventory.
search_accounting_and_DSR.DSR_requirement: DSR probability for the primary paired daily active-return series against the locked search-aware per-period null is at least 0.95 before validation-grade candidacy.
additional_search_forbidden: universe, timing, sizing, cost, benchmark, regime, or date-window selection
search_accounting_and_DSR.debug_run_rule: Any run that reads market returns is a logged trial even if labelled debugging; synthetic fixture tests are not trials.

dual_MinTRL.actual_recalculation: Use primary paired daily net active-return lags 1-5 from the prior opened stage. Report calendar observations, paired dates, holding overlap, time-effective observations, and joint independent-bet equivalents; raw trades never substitute for effective observations.
dual_MinTRL.kernel: lib/statistics.py minimum_track_record_length_falsify and minimum_track_record_length_validate; both receive only per-period Sharpe inputs.

falsification_decision_matrix.conditions: Actual primary active-return independent-bet equivalents are below 54048.
falsification_decision_matrix.decision: No falsification conclusion; remain E1 scope_restricted.

falsification_decision_matrix.conditions: Actual primary active-return independent-bet equivalents are at least 54048 and primary annualized active Sharpe is below 0.10 and primary-margin PSR is at most 0.05.
falsification_decision_matrix.decision: Falsify L-2 only after the required mechanism autopsy covers horizon diversification, signal transform, lag, noise, and cost.

falsification_decision_matrix.conditions: Any other falsification-stage result, missing data, integrity blocker, or failure to fund all validation nulls.
falsification_decision_matrix.decision: Remain E1 scope_restricted; do not open validation or promote a sensitivity.

falsification_decision_matrix.conditions: Primary annualized active Sharpe is at least 0.10, primary-margin PSR and DSR are each at least 0.95, actual primary active-return independent-bet equivalents fund both validation nulls at 216218, and inherited integrity, cost, regime, and adversarial-review gates pass.
falsification_decision_matrix.decision: Only then may L-2 become an E2 candidate; E2 promotion still requires a separate independent adversarial review.

validation_seal.unlock_rule: No L-2 validation access in B5.1. A later execution order must first pass the inherited L-1 data and validation boundary, fund every L-2 MinTRL_validate null using actual paired-active-return dependence, and receive separate owner approval and a new locked gate.

hard_stops: L-2 return, signal, portfolio, or backtest execution during B5.1
broker or provider request, credential use, paid action, paper trade, or real-money action
in-place change to any locked preregistration or validator after hashing
edge, E2, execution-quality, deployment, or real-money claim from B5.1
